translation.en: I am very busy with work today.

content.sentence: Hôm nay tôi rất ___ với công việc.

notes_md:
**bận rộn** = busy; having a lot to do and not much free time.

It’s an adjective, used to describe people (sometimes schedules or days). You often pair it with **với + noun** to say what someone is busy with.  


You can also say just **bận** in casual speech — it’s shorter and very common. Opposite idea: **rảnh** (free, not busy).
exampleSentencesTranslated: This week I am very busy with a lot of work at the office.
She is always busy so she doesn't have much time for her family.
I feel busy every time exams come close.

translation.en: I am very busy with work today.

content.sentence: Hôm nay tôi rất ___ với công việc.

example.en: I am very busy with work today.
example.vi: Hôm nay tôi rất bận rộn với công việc.
example.es: Hoy estoy muy ocupado con el trabajo.

definitions.vi: Có nhiều việc phải làm; không rảnh.
definitions.es: Tener mucho que hacer; no estar libre.
definitions.en: Having a lot to do; not free.

notes:
bận rộn = busy; having a lot to do and not much free time.

It’s an adjective, used to describe people (sometimes schedules or days). You often pair it with với + noun to say what someone is busy with.  

You can also say just bận in casual speech — it’s shorter and very common. Opposite idea: rảnh (free, not busy).
exampleSentences: Tuần này anh rất bận rộn với nhiều công việc ở văn phòng.
Chị ấy luôn bận rộn nên không có nhiều thời gian cho gia đình.
Em cảm thấy bận rộn mỗi khi kỳ thi đến gần.
